name: Spinach-Stuffed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 2 Cups Fresh Spinach Leaves, Washed, Dried, chopped in thin pieces
1/4 Cup Freshly Grated Parmesan or Romano Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Pierce each potato in two or three places.
3. Rub potatoes lightly with olive oil.
4. In a microwave-safe dish, arrange the potatoes in a circle with the thickest parts to the outside and cook on high power for 10 minutes, turning them every 3 minutes until they begin to whistle.
5. Bake in the oven at 375°F for 15-20 minutes until they can be easily pierced with a fork (or bake in a preheated 375°F oven for 45-60 minutes using the conventional method).
6. While the potatoes are baking, sauté the onion in a few drops of olive oil until soft.
7. Add the spinach and sauté about 30 seconds until soft, then remove from heat.
8. Mix the sautéed onion, spinach, salt, pepper, and buttermilk in a bowl (if using frozen potatoes, simply stir into onions and remove from heat).
9. When the potatoes are done, cut them in half lengthwise using pot holders and scoop out the flesh with a spoon.
10. Add the scooped-out potato flesh to the spinach/buttermilk mixture and mash together with a fork.
11. Arrange the skins on a baking sheet and spoon the mixture into each skin.
12. Sprinkle with the grated cheese and cayenne pepper.
13. Return to the oven at 375°F for about 20 minutes.
